evaluations.query: Set up a branching strategy for our new project
expected_behavior: Recommends GitHub Flow branching model for continuous delivery workflows
Uses issue/<number>-<description> branch naming convention for traceability
Enforces that the main branch is always in a deployable state
Requires PR workflow for protected branches

evaluations.query: I accidentally dropped my stash, how do I recover it?
expected_behavior: Uses git reflog to find the dropped stash
Provides step-by-step stash recovery commands using git fsck
Explains stash management best practices including naming and cleanup
Shows how to avoid future stash loss

evaluations.query: Should I merge or rebase my feature branch?
expected_behavior: Recommends rebase over merge for clean history
Explains conflict resolution strategies during interactive rebase operations
Uses force-with-lease for safe pushing after rebase
Warns against rebasing shared branches that others have pulled

evaluations.query: I committed to the wrong branch, how do I move it?
expected_behavior: Uses reflog to find the commit
Recommends cherry-pick to move the commit to the correct branch
Provides reset to remove from wrong branch
Distinguishes pushed vs unpushed commits with appropriate recovery strategies

evaluations.query: My branch has WIP and fixup commits, how do I clean up before pushing?
expected_behavior: Uses interactive rebase to squash WIP and fixup commits into clean atomic commits
Shows git commit --fixup and --autosquash workflow for incremental development cleanup
Keeps each final commit meaningful with a message explaining why not just what
Warns against rewriting published history that other developers have already pulled

evaluations.query: How do I configure Claude Code for a monorepo with separate API and web services?
expected_behavior: Uses --add-dir flag to include additional service directories in Claude Code context
Creates per-service CLAUDE.md files with framework patterns and test commands
Sets CLAUDE_CODE_ADDITIONAL_DIRECTORIES_CLAUDE_MD environment variable for full context loading
Keeps root CLAUDE.md for workspace-wide conventions and cross-service rules

evaluations.query: I need to undo my last commit but keep the changes staged for a different commit
expected_behavior: Uses git reset --soft HEAD~1 to undo the commit while keeping changes staged
Saves a backup reference with git rev-parse HEAD before any hard reset operation
Explains the three reset modes: soft keeps staged, mixed unstages, hard discards everything
Recommends git revert instead of reset for commits already pushed to shared branches

evaluations.query: My base PR got review feedback and now the dependent PRs are out of sync, how do I fix them?
expected_behavior: Rebases dependent branches in dependency order starting from the base branch first
Uses --force-with-lease instead of --force to prevent overwriting collaborators work
Retargets the next PR to main via gh pr edit after a base PR merges
Resolves rebase conflicts in the lowest branch first then cascades through the stack

evaluations.query: I need to implement a large auth feature but want small reviewable PRs, how do I split it?
expected_behavior: Breaks the feature into stacked PRs each under 400 lines targeting the previous PR branch
Numbers PRs clearly in titles like 1/3, 2/3, 3/3 for ordering visibility
Creates each PR with --base set to the previous PR branch for proper dependency chain
Includes a stack visualization table in the PR body showing order, status, and merge sequence
